title: Container not stopping or removed when console is terminated
body:
### Issue Description

Running Podman on Mac OS (Ventura) I have several VS Code tasks which launch container instances. I run them with the `--rm` argument in order to remove the containers when I close VS Code window which was working flawlessly until recently. Now the containers keep running in background.

At first I thought it was an issue with Code, but the problem can be replicated opening a terminal and closing the window the container will keep running in background.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Run a container with the command `podman run --rm {name}`
2. Open another terminal and launch `podman stats` and check that a new container instance is created and running
3. Close the terminal window when the instance was running, if a warning appears, insist.


### Describe the results you received

The launched container is still running in background as shown with `podman stats`

### Describe the results you expected

The launched container stopped, and removed if `--rm` parameter has been given.

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
podman run just forwards the signal to the container process, in case of closing a terminal that would most likely be SIGHUP, are you sure your container does not ignore the signal?

Alternatively it could be that your terminal is killing the podman run process right away so it cannot forward the signal.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Closing as I never got a reply
